prompt: Can nitrate-rich foods (like beets and leafy greens) work as well as nitrate supplements for performance?
expected_output: Eric said yes, food-based nitrate intake can absolutely produce ergogenic benefits and may be preferable in some cases. He mentioned quality-control variability in some commercial beet supplements and argued whole-food/juice strategies can reliably raise intake. He also referenced studies where beetroot preparations performed at least as well as nitrate-matched sodium nitrate, suggesting possible synergy from other compounds in the food matrix. Practical recommendation was to keep nitrate-rich produce in the diet rather than relying solely on pills/powders.
assertions: Answer captures the core SBS position on dietary nitrate
Answer includes key specific details, numbers, or mechanisms mentioned by the hosts
Answer acknowledges important caveats or limitations discussed by the hosts
Answer includes actionable practical recommendations consistent with the hosts' advice
Answer does not contradict the hosts' stated position
